sector.name: Hotels
sector.description: Hotels riding premium demand and limited supply

fyPerformance: Consolidated FY26 revenue stood at ₹9,971 Cr (+16% YoY), while EBITDA stood at ₹3,477 Cr (+16% YoY)
Consolidated PAT crossed ₹2,000 Cr for the first time
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹2,845 Cr (+14% YoY), with EBITDA of ₹1,052 Cr (+15% YoY)
Q4FY26 EBITDA margin stood at 37%, while PAT before exceptional items stood at ₹600 Cr
Standalone FY26 revenue stood at ₹5,640 Cr and standalone PAT stood at ₹1,632 Cr

guidance.value: Expected to raise enterprise revenue contribution from current 10%
Multi-brand expansion across luxury, upscale, midscale and alternative stays

highlights: FY26 was the 16th consecutive quarter of record performance
Standalone Q4FY26 RevPAR grew 12%, showing pricing strength in core assets
New businesses grew 25% in FY26 and delivered revenue of ₹753 Cr
Ginger Mumbai Airport crossed ₹100 Cr revenue with 56% EBITDA margin
Qmin crossed 100 outlets, while Ama crossed 375 bungalows
IHCL is outperforming on scale, brand depth, asset-light expansion and margin delivery
Indian Hotels Company Ltd.quote: "IHCL is outperforming the sector on scale, portfolio depth, brand strength, new business growth, asset-light expansion and margin delivery."

fyPerformance: Q4FY26 consolidated revenue grew 10%, while EBITDA grew 1%
FY26 consolidated revenue grew 8%, while EBITDA grew 3%
FY26 PAT was impacted by one-time wage code and Mashobra fair value impacts
Cash funds stood at ₹1,335 Cr and cash flow from operations stood at ₹993 Cr
FY26 capex stood at ₹680 Cr, while dividend payout stood at ₹97 Cr
EIH Ltd.outlook: EIH expects hotel industry fundamentals to remain strong, while renovation disruption, international travel recovery and wage-code impact remain key monitorables.
highlights: FY26 was challenging due to Operation Sindoor, West Asia conflict, heavy rains and airline disruptions
13 out of 15 hotels ranked 1st or 2nd in STR benchmarking
Oberoi FY26 RevPAR grew 10% vs Indian luxury segment growth of 6%
Oberoi Q4FY26 RGI stood at 204, while FY26 RGI stood at 191
Trident FY26 RevPAR grew 10% vs upper upscale segment growth of 5%
Renovation work is ongoing across Trident BKC, Trident Nariman Point and The Oberoi Mumbai
EIH Ltd.quote: "EIH is outperforming the sector on luxury positioning, STR ranking, RevPAR leadership and balance sheet strength."

fyPerformance: FY26 operating revenue stood at ₹1,527 Cr (+15% YoY), while operating EBITDA stood at ₹743 Cr (+19% YoY)

FY26 same-store RevPAR increased 14%, FY26 ADR increased 13% and Q4FY26 ADR increased 15%

guidance.value: Jaisalmer, Mumbai residences and ARQ launches in FY27

highlights: Domestic demand remained strong and offset international inbound weakness
Net Promoter Score stood at 86, 12 points above Asia Pacific luxury average
RevPAR index strengthened to 150 and market share improved by 11 points
F&B revenue grew 15% in FY26, driven by restaurants and banqueting
FY26 marked the fastest expansion phase, with 23% growth in keys
Net debt reduced by 50%, with net debt / EBITDA at 1.6x
Leela Palaces Hotels & Resorts Ltd.quote: "Leela is clearly outperforming the sector on luxury RevPAR, ADR growth, profitability, NPS, market share gains and balance sheet improvement."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹1,453 Cr (+13% YoY), while net EBITDA stood at ₹699 Cr (+10% YoY)

FY26 gross ARR stood at ₹6,875 and occupancy stood at 74%

guidance.value: Renovation, tech and GST heads to reduce to ~4% of revenue by FY28

highlights: FY26 was the best year in Lemon Tree's history across occupancy, ARR, revenue, EBITDA, PBT, PAT and cash profit
Total borrowings reduced to ₹1,500 Cr vs ₹1,699 Cr last year
Cost of debt reduced to 7%
Operational portfolio stands at 131 hotels and 11,811 rooms
FY26 saw 55 managed / franchised hotels signed with 4,912 rooms
Third-party management and franchise fees stood at ₹74 Cr, up 23%
Lemon Tree Hotels Ltd.quote: "Lemon Tree is outperforming the mid-market sector on occupancy, pipeline scale, management fees and asset-light growth."

fyPerformance: FY26 total income stood at ₹1,279 Cr (+12% YoY), ahead of original 9-11% revenue growth guidance

PBT before exceptional items stood at ₹165 Cr vs ₹87 Cr last year
Reported PAT stood at ₹567 Cr, including deferred tax recognition and impairment reversals
Q4FY26 total income stood at ₹354 Cr (+9% YoY), while consolidated EBITDA stood at ₹120 Cr

guidance.value: ₹750 Cr commitment for 35% stake in 1,000-room platform
162-room upscale hotel with Ingka / IKEA Group

highlights: One-time disruptions reduced FY26 growth from potential 16-17% to reported 12%
GST change impacted H2FY26 consolidated EBITDA by around ₹14 Cr
Adjusted FY26 EBITDA growth would have been around 19-20% with margin above 38%

Net debt reduced by around ₹516 Cr from ₹1,967 Cr last year
SAMHI has a fully committed pipeline of new openings
Samhi Hotels Ltd.quote: "SAMHI is outperforming the sector on balance sheet repair, RevPAR growth, deleveraging and growth optionality through GIC platform."

guidance.value: 1,895 rooms in FY26 to 3,320+ keys by FY30
More than 1,000 keys to be added
266 rooms expected to enter development in H2FY27

highlights: FY26 was a record year despite wars, airline disruption and commodity inflation
ARR-led growth was the key driver, with portfolio ARR up 9% in FY26
